title: Winey Russian Beef Pilaf
description: A comforting, wine-infused beef pilaf with crunchy toasted almonds and a nostalgic Russian flair, dairy free and quick to prepare.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced beef and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté onion and carrot until softened, about 4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, thyme, and bay leaf, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in 200 ml red wine, deglaze the pan, and simmer until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the sevaiiya grain and stir to coat with the wine mixture, then return the beef to the pan.
Cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until the grain is tender and liquid absorbed, about 20 minutes.
Sprinkle toasted almonds over the pilaf, season with salt and pepper, and serve hot.
